content:
- Finished task-02-quickstart-docs and pushed commit 3fe1a62 to origin/master.
- Added README.md as the canonical quickstart, updated examples/k8s/README.md to point back to it, marked the Ralph task/plan complete, switched tasks, committed, and pushed.
- Validation evidence: make check, make lint, make test, docker compose quickstart flow, benchmark API curls, docker build -t pg_gobench:local ., and direct scratch-image verification on port 18080 all passed.

- Read prior Ralph progress and current task summaries.
- Found two active tasks: story-09 UI task is failing but unblocked because story-08 quickstart docs is done; story-99 manual verification is failing but blocked by story-09.
- Next: refresh task summary, confirm choose-task rules and repo stack, then write the selected task path to .ralph/current_task.txt.

- Refreshed Ralph task summaries and read the choose-task rules plus the two remaining task files.
- Confirmed actual repo shape is a Go service (`cmd/pg_gobench`, `internal/*`, Makefile, tests, docs/examples) and not a copied framework project.
- Selection result: no unblocked failing bugs exist; story-09 UI task is the only unblocked failing task; story-99 manual verification remains blocked by story-09. Next worker should execute story-09 UI task.

- Read prior progress, loaded the mandatory tdd and improve-code-boundaries skill instructions, and resolved current task path to story-09 standalone HTML control page.
- Confirmed this turn needs to follow the Ralph task file state before making code changes.
- Next: read the task file, inspect its story directory and plan status, then either create/update the plan or execute it.

- Read the story-09 task, repo planning conventions, HTTP routes, benchmark option/result contracts, and Makefile gates.
- Identified a real design constraint for the standalone HTML page: opening from disk means browser fetches hit cross-origin rules, so the JSON API needs explicit CORS + OPTIONS support or the page cannot honestly call POST/JSON endpoints.
- Next: create the task plan file with that boundary decision, update the task with the plan path, leave the plan at TO BE VERIFIED, and stop as instructed.

- Created the story-09 plan file and linked it from the task file.
- Finalized the design around a single standalone HTML file plus one centralized CORS/preflight layer in internal/httpserver so a file:// page can honestly call the existing JSON API.
- Next: start execution from the NOW EXECUTE plan, implement the HTML page and HTTP browser-compatibility changes, then verify with make check/make lint/make test and real browser interaction against a running API.

- Read prior progress, current task pointer, and the required improve-code-boundaries and tdd skill instructions.
- Confirmed story-09 already has a plan from the previous context; next step is to read the task file and linked plan to determine whether this turn should execute or stop.
- If the plan says NOW EXECUTE, proceed with red-green implementation and validation; if not, update plan state only and quit immediately.

- Read the story-09 task and confirmed the linked plan is in NOW EXECUTE state, so this turn must implement and validate rather than re-plan.
- Next I am inspecting the HTTP server, existing endpoint contracts, and current tests so the standalone HTML page can call the real API without adding template/static coupling.
- I need to verify whether a single mux-level CORS/preflight layer is enough, then add the browser page against the existing benchmark payloads.

- Inspected internal/httpserver and benchmark option decoding. The cleanest boundary change is a single server wrapper that adds CORS headers and handles OPTIONS preflight for the existing routes.
- The standalone page can stay a single HTML file with inline CSS/JS because the JSON API already exposes everything needed; no template serving or backend UI layer is necessary.
- Next: patch server/tests for browser access, then add examples/standalone-control-page.html, then run make check/make lint/make test and honest browser verification.

- Implemented the first pass: server mux wrapper for CORS/preflight, coverage in internal/httpserver tests, the standalone HTML page, and a README pointer.
- Next I am running formatter and targeted HTTP tests before the repo-wide gates so I can catch contract mismatches cheaply.
- After that I still need real browser verification against a live API, which is the primary proof for this task.

- make check, make lint, and make test passed. The remaining required validation is the honest browser/manual path against a running API.
- Next I am checking which browser or browser-automation tool exists locally, then I will start the API via the documented quickstart path and exercise the standalone page from disk.
- After browser verification, I still need the final boundary sanity pass, task file status update, task switch, commit, and push.

- Installed a real browser automation path using Playwright Chromium because no browser binary was preinstalled.
- Next I am bringing up the documented Docker Compose stack and will verify the standalone file over file:// against the live API once health/readiness are green.
- I still need to clean up the stack after verification and then finish the Ralph task-completion workflow.

- Real browser verification passed with Playwright Chromium against the live Docker Compose API using the file:// standalone page.
- Verified from the browser context: health, readiness, state, start, alter, stop, results, in-page metrics fetch, and opening /metrics in a new tab.
- Next: final boundary sanity check, update the Ralph task metadata to passed, switch task, commit all files, push, and stop immediately.

- Updated the story-09 task file to done with passes=true and checked acceptance criteria.
- Next I am running .ralph/task_switch.sh exactly as required, then I will tear down the local Compose verification stack, review the final diff, commit all files, push, and stop.
- Browser/manual proof is complete, so only repo bookkeeping remains.
